conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
A bit off. I get tired easily, and sometimes my head feels heavy. But I have no idea what is causing it.
You mentioned getting tired easily—has that been affecting your sleep at all?
Yeah, a bit. I wake up in the middle of the night, and then I just lie there for a bit before I can fall back asleep.
When you're lying awake at night, do you find your mind wandering to things you used to enjoy?
Yeah, a bit. I think about playing the guitar, but I haven't picked it up in ages. It's just something to distract me from being awake.
When you think about the future, does it feel like something you're looking forward to?
Not really. It's all just a bit of a blur right now. I just want to get through the next few months and figure things out.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It's just... weird. I used to know what I was doing, and now I don't. It feels like I'm just drifting.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yeah, definitely. I'll start something, and then I just get distracted easily. It's like I'm not even trying.
When you get distracted like that, do you find yourself feeling more on edge or irritable?
Not really. I just get annoyed with myself for not focusing. But I try not to let it get to me too much.
Can you tell me more about how that has been affecting your day-to-day?
It makes the time drag. I just want to get things done so I can relax.
You mentioned wanting to get things done so you can relax—what does that relaxation look like for you when you finally get there?
Just watching TV or playing games. Something to take my mind off things.
When you're watching TV or playing games to take your mind off things, does it actually help you feel better, or is it more just a way to pass the time?
It's just a way to pass the time. It doesn't really help me relax or anything.
How have you been feeling lately?
I’m fine, just a bit tired from work. Nothing major, just need a good night’s sleep.
You mentioned being tired from work—has that made it hard to wind down at night?
Yeah, definitely. I usually try to relax for a bit, but my brain just keeps going.
When your mind is racing like that, does it make it harder to get into things you normally enjoy?
Yeah, a bit. I usually just scroll through my phone until I’m tired enough to sleep.
When you're lying there with your mind racing, does it ever make you feel down on yourself?
Sometimes. I think, "Why can't I just turn off my brain?" But it’s not a big deal.
Can you tell me more about how that has been affecting your day-to-day?
Just small stuff, like missing deadlines or forgetting things. It’s annoying, but I get it done eventually.
You mentioned missing deadlines—does that ever make you feel disappointed in yourself?
A bit. I hate being late, but I know it happens sometimes.
When you're dealing with that frustration, do you find yourself getting more irritable with people around you?
Not usually. I just get a bit snappy with my family, but they know I’m just stressed.
When you're feeling snappy with your family, what does that usually look like?
I just snap at them for no reason, like, "Stop talking." Then I feel bad and apologize.
After you apologize, how does that usually make you feel?
Better. I know I’m being stupid, and they know it too. We just laugh it off.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
Just embarrassing, I guess. I don't like being that person, but I know it happens.
When you say you don't like being "that person," what kind of person do you feel like you are in those moments?
Just a bit short. I hate being short with people. It's not a good look.
When you feel like you're being short with people, does that ever make you want to pull back from them?
No, not really. I just try to apologize and make it right.
When you're trying to make things right after snapping, does it ever feel like you're just going through the motions?
Sometimes. I feel like I’m just saying the words, but I know it’s the right thing to do.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
Just a bit overwhelmed, I guess. But I know I’m not alone, so I’m trying to just take it one day at a time.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
I just feel a bit tired, that’s all. I think I just need to get some better sleep.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
Just that I'm a bit stressed, but I’m managing it.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
No, I think that’s it. Just the usual stuff.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
No, I think I’m good. Just tired.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
No, I think that’s it. Just a bit tired.
You've mentioned being tired a few times—has that feeling of tiredness been more of a physical exhaustion or a mental one?
I think it’s a bit of both. Just feeling a bit run down, you know?
How have you been feeling lately?
A bit off. I get tired easily, and sometimes my mind just goes blank. I feel like I should be more productive, but I just can't seem to focus.
You mentioned getting tired easily—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It’s pretty annoying. I used to be able to do a lot without getting worn out, but now I’m just exhausted by lunchtime. I try to push through, but it’s hard to stay motivated when I’m that tired.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It’s frustrating. I feel like I’m just coasting through the day. I used to enjoy my work, but now it just feels like a chore because I’m so tired all the time.
You mentioned work feels like a chore now—has that lack of enjoyment spilled over into other things you used to like doing?
Yeah, pretty much. I used to like going out with friends, but now I just don't feel like it. I get anxious thinking about socializing when I’m still tired from the day.
When you think about the future, does it feel like things will get better, or does it seem kind of hopeless?
I don't know. I guess I just want to figure out why I’m feeling this way and get my energy back.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yeah, definitely. I get sidetracked really easily now. I'll start something and then just stop because I lose interest or get bored.
You mentioned getting anxious about socializing—has that feeling of being on edge shown up in other parts of your day?
Sometimes. I get a bit jittery when I'm waiting for things to happen, like when I’m waiting for a meeting to start.
You mentioned feeling jittery while waiting—does that feeling ever make it hard to just sit still or relax?
Yeah, it does. I find myself pacing around the office or tapping my feet because I just can’t sit still.
When you're pacing or tapping your feet, what's usually going through your mind?
Just that I should be doing something productive, I guess. I feel like I’m wasting time or being inefficient.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
Just that I feel a bit lost, I suppose. I used to know what I wanted to do and how to get there, but now I just feel like I’m drifting.
How have you been feeling lately?
Irritable. I get annoyed at the smallest things. Like if someone is talking loudly on the phone or the traffic is slow. It’s just little things, but it gets on my nerves.
Has that irritability made it harder to wind down or get a good night's sleep?

Yeah, definitely. I lie there at night and my mind just won't stop. I think about everything I need to do tomorrow, or what someone said at work, and I can't switch off. I just lie there going over it all in my head.
When your mind is racing like that, does it make it hard to get excited about things you usually enjoy?

Yeah, it does. I used to love watching football on Sundays, but now I just can't focus on the game. I'll be watching and I'll think, 'I need to get the washing done' or 'I forgot to pick up milk.' It takes the fun out of it.
When you're lying there at night with your mind racing, do you ever find yourself being really hard on yourself about things?

Constantly. I beat myself up over stupid stuff. Like if I spill something or forget to turn off the stove, I'll be going over it in my head for hours. It's just... I don't know, it's like I'm trying to punish myself.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It feels... out of control. Like I've lost my grip on things. I used to be able to handle stress, but now it just feels like it's running me.
How do you see things going for you in the near future?
I don't know. I just feel like I'm stuck in a rut. I don't know how to get out of it.
You mentioned feeling stuck in a rut—has that feeling made you more restless or on edge during the day?
Yeah, definitely. I get fidgety. I'll be sitting at my desk and I'll just want to get up and walk out the door. I don't know where I'd go, I just want to get away from everything.
When you get that urge to just get up and walk out, what's usually going through your mind right before that happens?
Just frustration. Frustration with everything. Work, life, just... everything. It's like I'm trapped and I don't know how to get out of it.
When you say you feel trapped, what does that feel like in your body?
It's a knot in my chest. It's like I'm holding my breath, waiting for something to break.
When you feel that knot in your chest, does it make it hard to focus on tasks or make decisions?
Yeah, it does. I'll be trying to think about something and my mind will just go blank. I'll stare at the screen and I won't know what to do.
How have you been feeling lately?

Exhausted. I've been working a lot of overtime to get everything done on time, and my social life is basically non-existent. I just want to sit on the couch and watch Netflix without thinking about spreadsheets for five minutes.
Can you tell me more about how that has been affecting your day-to-day?
I wake up, go to work, come home, eat dinner, and then crash. I don't have the energy to talk to friends or family anymore. I'm just... empty.
How have you been sleeping?
Not great. I usually wake up around 2 or 3 in the morning, and then I just lie there for a bit. It’s like my brain is trying to keep me awake.
You mentioned just wanting to sit on the couch and watch Netflix—is that something you actually look forward to, or is it more just to pass the time?
Honestly? It’s just to pass the time. I don't even really care what I'm watching. I just need to sit still for a bit and not think about work.
Has there been anything lately that you've actually looked forward to doing?
Not really. I used to love playing guitar, but I haven't touched it in months. I just don't have the energy to care about anything that takes effort.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It feels like I'm just waiting for something to happen. I used to be the guy who had a plan for the weekend, but now I just show up and see what happens.
Been doing anything you enjoy lately?
I went to a friend’s birthday party last weekend. It was nice to see people laughing and having a good time, but I was mostly just there to drink a beer and nod along.
How do you see things going for you in the near future?
Honestly? I don't see things changing anytime soon. I'm just going to keep working and trying to stay awake until I can retire.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yeah. It’s like my brain is a browser with too many tabs open. I try to focus on one thing, but it just keeps jumping to another thing.
With your brain feeling so overloaded, do you find yourself getting more irritable or on edge lately?
Definitely. I get annoyed at the little things that used to roll off my back. Like, I'll be in a meeting and someone will say something stupid, and I'll just want to scream.
When you feel that urge to scream, what do you usually end up doing instead?
I usually just leave the meeting. I don't have the energy to deal with it, so I just tell them I have another call and walk out.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
Just that it’s hard. I used to be the guy who had it all together, but now I’m just trying to survive.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
I don't know. Maybe just that I wish I could turn it off. I feel like I'm stuck in this never-ending cycle of exhaustion and boredom.
You mentioned feeling stuck in a cycle—what would it look like to break out of it?
Honestly? I have no idea. I just feel like I’m stuck in quicksand and I don't know how to get out.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
I guess just that I'm tired of being tired. I wish I could just wake up and feel like I have a purpose again.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
No, I think that’s it. Just... I don't know how to fix it, and it's hard to admit that.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
No, that’s enough. I just... I don't know.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
No.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
No.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
No.
How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It’s like watching someone else live my life. I look at people moving around, and I feel like I’m just stuck on the sidelines.
You mentioned feeling stuck on the sidelines—has that made it hard to care about things you used to enjoy?
Yeah, it has. I used to love reading, but now it feels pointless. I don't know why I'm wasting my time with it when I can't even enjoy it.
When you say reading feels pointless, what does that make you think about yourself?
It makes me think I’m done. Like, I've lived my life and now I’m just going through the motions.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
It’s been building for a while. After the divorce, things changed, and I couldn’t get the old me back.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
The evenings. I sit there, and I have nothing to look forward to. No one to call, no plans... just silence.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I feel like I'm just waiting for something to happen, but I have no idea what that is.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
I don't know if it's giving up or just accepting that this is my life now. Either way, it feels like the end of something, even if I don't know what that something is.
When you say this will end soon, are you talking about ending your life?
No, I don't think that far. I just mean this feeling will pass eventually, and I'll find a way to move forward.
Have you thought about specific steps or a specific way you would do it?
No, I haven't. I just know that I can't keep living like this, and I don't know what else to do.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to get excited about stuff you used to enjoy during the day?
Yeah, it does. I used to love hiking, but now I just can't seem to muster the energy to go out there and enjoy it.
When you think about those things you could have done differently, how does that make you feel about yourself?
It makes me feel really stupid. I know I should be stronger than this, but I just can’t seem to get my head around it.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
It started maybe a month ago. It just felt like a switch flipped and suddenly I was wondering where I went wrong.
You mentioned feeling like a switch flipped—what does that feel like for you when you think about the future?
It’s like looking at a road that used to be easy, but now it’s just a long, dark path. I don't know how to get back to where I was.
When you're looking at that long, dark path, does it make it hard to focus on things you need to do right now?
Yeah, it does. I just sit there at my desk and can't seem to get my head on straight.
When you're sitting at your desk and can't focus, do you find yourself feeling more on edge or restless?
Yeah, I get really fidgety. I just want to get up and walk away, but I know I need to stay and figure things out.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
Just everything. Work, relationships... everything feels flat and grey. I don't know how to turn that back on.
When you say everything feels flat and grey, what does a typical day look like for you now?
It’s just going through the motions. I wake up, I go to work, I come home, and I just sit there on the couch feeling empty.
How have you been feeling lately?
I’m okay, I suppose. Just a bit tired. I haven’t been sleeping well, but I think it’s just stress from work.
You mentioned not sleeping well—what’s been keeping you up?
Just random things, like work emails or stuff I need to do tomorrow. My mind won't switch off, so I just end up checking my phone until I'm tired.
When you're not lying awake thinking about work, do you find yourself wanting to do things you normally enjoy?
Not really. I just want to get through the day, be honest. I used to love reading or watching a movie, but right now it just feels like a chore.
When you say you just want to get through the day, what does that feel like for you?
Just drained. I don't have the energy for much. I just get home, make dinner, and go to bed. It's not how I want to live, but I just need to get through it until things settle down.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It's just a low-level tiredness, like I'm running on empty. I don't have the motivation to do much, so I just do the bare minimum until I can rest again.
When you think about this just being until things settle down, what does settling down look like for you?
Just getting back to normal, I suppose. I used to enjoy my free time, and I miss that. I just want to be able to relax again and not feel like I'm just going through the motions.
You mentioned missing the way you used to enjoy your free time—how does that make you feel about yourself?
A bit frustrated, to be honest. I feel like I've lost my spark a bit. I just want to get back to being me, not this tired, worn-out person.
When you think about getting back to being "you," what does that person look like?
Someone who can enjoy a quiet night in or a walk. Someone who doesn't have to think about work all the time. I just miss having a sense of balance, you know?
How do you see things going for you in the near future?
I hope things start to settle down soon. I just need to get through this patch and get my energy back.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yeah, definitely. I'll be in the middle of something and my mind will just wander off. It's frustrating because I know I'm capable of focusing, but right now I just can't seem to get there.
Can you tell me more about how that has been affecting your day-to-day?
Just little things, like misreading emails or forgetting appointments. It's not a big deal, but it's annoying because I know I’m better than that.
Have you found yourself getting emotional more easily lately?
Yeah, I've been a bit short with people. I know it's just stress, but it's not like me. I just need to find my balance again.
When you feel short with people, what kind of thoughts are going through your mind?
Just frustration and irritation. I know it's not how I want to be, but I just feel like I'm running on empty.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
Just that I miss my old self. I miss being able to relax and enjoy things. I just want to get back to being me again.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
Just that I'm tired of feeling like this. I want to be able to look forward to the weekend again, not just be relieved it's here.
